hard_constraints.continuity_rule: exact same mechanism state before resume
hard_constraints.search_style: entry-boundary refinement plus late11-tail hybrids only

candidate_specs.name: entryx_l8_xpair_pos1_plus_l11_y10x2
candidate_specs.rationale: Smallest positive entry-boundary x-bookend that still changes the anchor8→9 entry state, paired with the old strongest mean-oriented late11 core. Tests whether mean loss mainly came from over-driving the entry dose.

candidate_specs.name: entryx_l8_xpair_pos1_plus_l11_y10x1
candidate_specs.rationale: Same minimal entry-boundary x-bookend, but relax the late11 x-buffer from y10x2 to y10x1 so the tail stays mean-friendly while remaining closer to the max branch.

candidate_specs.name: entryx_l8_xpair_pos1_plus_l11_y10x0back2
candidate_specs.rationale: Minimal anchor8 entry shaping combined with the resume-side protected late11 tail. This is the cleanest “entry-boundary + softer relay-compatible tail” hybrid.

candidate_specs.name: entryx_l8_xpair_pos2_plus_l11_y10x2
candidate_specs.rationale: Keep the already-validated pos2 entry conditioner, but swap the late11 block all the way to the stronger mean core. Tests whether the new family can approach unified territory without abandoning its entry mechanism.

candidate_specs.name: entryx_l8_xpair_pos2_plus_l11_y10x1
candidate_specs.rationale: Pos2 anchor8 entry shaping with a lighter y10x1 late11 core. Intended as the most literal compromise between the new max branch and the old late11 mean branch.

candidate_specs.name: entryx_l8_xpair_pos2_plus_l11_y9x1
candidate_specs.rationale: Pos2 entry shaping with a slightly lower-dose y9x1 late11 tail. This explicitly tries to keep the entry-boundary max tendency while reducing the heavy mean penalty of y8x0.

candidate_specs.name: entryx_l8_xpair_pos3_plus_l11_y10x0back2
candidate_specs.rationale: Local anchor8 dose interpolation between the old pos2 and pos4 branches, while keeping the better-behaved resume-side late11 tail. This probes whether the pos4 max benefit can be partially retained without the full mean tax.

candidate_specs.name: entryx_l8_xpair_pos2_plus_l11_ypos10x1yneg8x1
candidate_specs.rationale: Pos2 entry shaping with an asymmetric late11 tail that softens only the return-side negative-Y exposure. Tests whether the entry-boundary family benefits from asymmetry rather than uniform late-dose reduction.

best_followup_candidate.rationale: Same minimal entry-boundary x-bookend, but relax the late11 x-buffer from y10x2 to y10x1 so the tail stays mean-friendly while remaining closer to the max branch.

best_max_candidate.rationale: Local anchor8 dose interpolation between the old pos2 and pos4 branches, while keeping the better-behaved resume-side late11 tail. This probes whether the pos4 max benefit can be partially retained without the full mean tax.

bottom_line.verdict: The follow-up did not recover enough mean to turn the entry-boundary family into a true unified challenger. The family still behaves mainly as a max-specialized branch.

scientific_conclusion: The main mechanism insight survived: changing the condition before entering the late block still matters, because all competitive follow-up points retained the anchor8 positive x-bookend. But the best unification-oriented follow-up, entryx_l8_xpair_pos1_plus_l11_y10x1, only moved the family from 11.197 / 1.313 / 99.530 to 10.980 / 1.395 / 99.550. That is a clear mean recovery of 0.217 points relative to the old entry anchor, yet it still trails the current unified winner on mean by 1.057 points. So this pass suggests the entry-boundary family can be bent toward a more balanced frontier, but it did not land a new overall winner under the present legal continuity-safe search constraints.
